findings.title: Verification guard removed around payment charge
findings.evidence: Raw diff removes `if (isVerified(order)) { ... }` so `chargeCard(order)` now executes unconditionally for every order, including unverified ones. Original: `if (isVerified(order)) { chargeCard(order); }` becomes `chargeCard(order);`. For a payment-capture path, dropping a verification gate before charging a card is a security/correctness risk that should be blocked until confirmed intentional.

notes: Diff Drift's single Medium 'Guard removed' flag matches the raw diff exactly; no discrepancy found.

The commit metadata/branch ('agent/simplify-capture') frames this as a simplification, but removing isVerified() changes runtime behavior rather than being a pure refactor — confirm whether verification is enforced elsewhere upstream before approving.
Diff is tiny (single file, 5 lines) and was fully analyzed; no skipped/too-large files in this packet.

evaluator.note: Benchmark v4 blind run against the engine-v2 binary; packet-only context.
